texto: ...it really struck me that, just as people might look at me and never imagine I'd worked as a prostitute, they must look at some of those girls and see only the alienation and disaffection that hides their on fears and hurt.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Sophie Hayes sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “...it really struck me that, just as people might look at me and never imagine I'd worked as a pr…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a trabalho, alienation, disaffection, útil em momentos de trabalho e disaffection.

frase_original: ...it really struck me that, just as people might look at me and never imagine I'd worked as a prostitute, they must look at some of those girls and see only the alienation and disaffection that hides their on fears and hurt.

texto: Part of the problem was that I couldn't seem to get past the fact that I hadn't tried to escape from Kas. Even in France, when he'd left me on my own for several days, I'd carried on working [as a prostitute] and doing all the things he'd told me to d. And although I knew that it was because of the fear he'd so carefully and deliberately instilled in me, I still felt as though I'd somehow colluded in what had happened to me - despite knowing, deep down, that nothing could have been further from the truth.
explicacao: Citação de Sophie Hayes sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Part of the problem was that I couldn't seem to get past the fact that I hadn't tried to escape f…” articula reflexao, abuse, fear em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e fear.

Even in France, when he'd left me on my own for several days, I'd carried on working [as a prostitute] and doing all the things he'd told me to d. And although I knew that it was because of the fear he'd so carefully and deliberately instilled in me, I still felt as though I'd somehow colluded in what had happened to me - despite knowing, deep down, that nothing could have been further from the truth.
